text:
Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) has the potential to transform wireless systems with more efficient spectrum reuse and hardware reduction. ISAC is expected to be a key component in future 5G-Advanced (5G-A) and 6G systems, which are designed to support the proliferation of new intelligent use cases requiring high-speed communication and accurate environmental sensing. All these can be realized by designing the right characteristics of the ISAC signal.

ISAC signals in the context of modern mobile communication systems have attracted a tremendous amount of attention and in this paper, the signal design, processing algorithms and optimization methods are all highlighted. The signal design has attracted a lot of research activities with new designs emerging for 5G, 5G-A, and 6G systems. Moreover, a large amount of research activities focused on the radar processing of the ISAC signals for obtaining the extracted sensing information such as channel information matrix, spectrum lines estimation and super-resolution methods. Finally, other optimization issues such as PAPR reduction, interference mitigation and design of adaptive signal structures have drawn the attentions of many researchers. By summary, this paper discusses theoretical and practical performance benchmarks for ISAC technologies in the context of future cellular networks.
